title: Effects of Reis Cloning again On Her Lifespan
display_title: Effects of Reis Cloning again On Her Lifespan
wikitext:
[]: Effects of Rei's Cloning (again?) On Her Lifespan             Quote Post by EveryoneGotTurnedIntoTang » Tue Nov 15, 2011 7:53 pm Did some reading up on cloning, and had a small theory forming in my mind. Feel free to correct any errors I make. Also feel free to direct me to any threads that discuss subjects similar to this if they exist already. So as we all know, Rei is a clone of Yui, with the soul of Lilith (duh). Though the exact nature of her cloning isn't exactly explored in the series (Rubber Science and all), in my mind, it goes like this: 1) An unfertilized ovum from Lilith is taken, and has its nucleus removed. 2) A nucleus from one of Yui's cells, which are kept in storage or something, is implanted into the Lilithian egg. 3) The egg is stimulated, and begins dividing. 4) Once it develops into an embryo, it is implanted into a female. My guess is that they probably implanted it into Lilith, although the possibility of surrogate mothers is possible. Not exactly a geneticist here. 5) Ta da. Rei clone! Now, to get to the main focus of this topic. From what I read, clones can be less healthy than the original. In the series, we see that Rei takes medication. Of course, you all knew that. If Rei was cloned from a sample of Yui's DNA around the time she got absorbed into EVA Unit-01, then can we assume that this has led to Rei having a shorter lifespan than normal human beings? This is based on the famous Dolly experiment, but if Rei was cloned from old DNA, then that can shorten her lifespan, considering shaved telomeres and all. A shorter lifespan wouldn't matter much in the grand scheme of things (since Rei joins with Lilith again in EoE), but could it be possible? And factoring in Rei's physical conditions and the strain it causes on her body, could it be that Rei has a significantly shorter lifespan than the rest of the cast? Say, 30-40 years?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Nov 15, 2011 8:11 pm My opinion of the cloning process was always the sort of "put some gunk in a test tube and let it grow" sort of thing, rather than anything scientifically plausible. Furthermore, we don't know if Lilith even has ovaries, or a womb (unless it's in her head, and that's where Unit-01 hangs out in EoE), and it would be incredibly infeasible to find and later silence (or murder) a whole lot of surrogate mothers to grow these clones in. As for clone deterioration, that seems to relate more to the integrity of her AT field rather than the integrity of her cellular structure, although the two could be related.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 16, 2011 12:47 am Rei seems to just be fully formed out of PWM-gunk, instead of anything scientifically probable. And because of what she's made of in the first place, she might not HAVE a lifespan.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Nov 16, 2011 12:48 am ^This.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Nov 16, 2011 8:57 am If Rei is made out of Lilith goo and her continued existence is totally dependent on her AT Field, the medication she takes might be placebos, as in "these pills keep you alive, be a good girl and keep taking them and don't spontaneously turn into a puddle while you do, thank you".

[]:             Quote Post by EveryoneGotTurnedIntoTang » Wed Nov 16, 2011 3:36 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Rei seems to just be fully formed out of PWM-gunk, instead of anything scientifically probable. And because of what she's made of in the first place, she might not HAVE a lifespan.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Nov 16, 2011 6:25 pm She can project an AT-Field of unparalleled strength and instantly regenerate a lost limb, both of which imply there's more to her than a normal human. If she can regenerate a whole limb she probably doesn't age. She'd either reach full adult development and never change or else she doesn't age at all, as she isn't meant to live to adulthood anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Nov 16, 2011 8:49 pm Chuckman wrote: If Rei is made out of Lilith goo and her continued existence is totally dependent on her AT Field, the medication she takes might be placebos, as in "these pills keep you alive, be a good girl and keep taking them and don't spontaneously turn into a puddle while you do, thank you".

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Nov 17, 2011 11:56 am She does, but something (presumably loyalty to Gendo) keeps her from acting on it. Unless something happened off screen that activated her ability to generate a field or it was a byproduct of being moved into a new body, Rei essentially had godlike powers and intimate knowledge of what was going on in the depths of Nerv the whole time. Something prevented her from acting on that.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 17, 2011 2:22 pm It's worth noting that despite wanting to die, Rei wants her death to be "excused" first. She wants to be useful first and foremost before she fades away. Her loyalty to the Ikaris is basically keeping her off the Grim Reaper's list.

[]:             Quote Post by EveryoneGotTurnedIntoTang » Thu Nov 17, 2011 3:39 pm Chuckman wrote: She can project an AT-Field of unparalleled strength and instantly regenerate a lost limb, both of which imply there's more to her than a normal human. If she can regenerate a whole limb she probably doesn't age. She'd either reach full adult development and never change or else she doesn't age at all, as she isn't meant to live to adulthood anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 17, 2011 6:10 pm EveryoneGotTurnedIntoTang wrote: Also, what kind of "strength" are you referring to? Granted, she's able to negate Kaworu's AT Field in eps 24, but apparently her AT Field doesn't take the same form as an Angel's (i.e. can physically absorb damage). This can probably be attributed to her lack of a S2 organ. Of course, bringing Kaworu into this conversation will only make things even more complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Thu Nov 17, 2011 7:42 pm Wouldn't this be completely irrelevant to Gendo anyway? By the time Rei was of an "older age", third impact would have already happened and her job would have been done. So even her creation involved cutting short her lifespan, I doubt it was really a concern to anyone involved.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Nov 17, 2011 9:37 pm Chuckman wrote: She does, but something (presumably loyalty to Gendo) keeps her from acting on it. Unless something happened off screen that activated her ability to generate a field or it was a byproduct of being moved into a new body, Rei essentially had godlike powers and intimate knowledge of what was going on in the depths of Nerv the whole time. Something prevented her from acting on that.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Fri Nov 18, 2011 4:56 am Okay, I am assuming that the creator was assuming that by 2015 we would have perfected cloning. After all, we still don't have buildings that lower into the ground or giant lazer beams. Just remember, yesterdays science fiction can and usually does become today's science fact. Maybe someone will perfect cloning in the next 4 years.

[]:             Quote Post by Xacebans » Sat Nov 19, 2011 1:28 pm I don't think that in Rei was meant to last, so I doubt Gendo and the others really cared about keeping her body alive for long.. I thought of a different question about teh Reis: If they're all just bodies floating around in an aquarium, why do they all have short hair? After living there for years, wouldn't they have longer hair than that? Or would...I dunno, Nerv officials give 'em the occasional haircut?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Nov 20, 2011 2:46 am Who knows. It's probably just something they didn't think about when they animated the scene.

[]: LASERZ!!!! YERRRAAA!!!!!!!! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Nov 20, 2011 4:03 am @supershinjiasukashipper : 4 years? Screw that sh*t, we have giant frickin' lasers right now . But I'd honestly prefer an Ion cannon, IN SPACE! @Topic: I feel like ZOMG, ANGEL SUPER POWERZ!1!!1! Is somehow relevant. I can't find the picture dammit, "Link Man" is dead...

[]:             Quote Post by Gecko state » Thu Nov 24, 2011 2:19 am Could it be that Gendo used Yui DNA and is pretty much trying to revive Yui. when Gendo tries to use Rei and retaliates my thoughts are that Gendo was trying to bring back Yui in the egg of Lilith. Because all souls of humans are retained in the egg and that are my thoughts behind what Gendo`s intentions with Rei`s. Not only that but Rei could pilot the eve00 and that the experiments that she died in always had me wondering about that Yui died in. Also in the first few episodes Shinji remembers a smell but cant place it and he thinks it i thinks that he recognizes the smell of his mother. Also most of all in several situations Gendo is strongly concerned with Rei and her welfare we all remember the burns Gendo receives. Rei is almost like a daughter in that he shares some of his wife's genetic material. Alternatively it could be read as ensuring that his "investment" survives for Rei is apparently Gendo’s gateway to controlling Instrumentality. my number one thought is what happened to Rei in ep 1 it would explain so many things testing a fight but never the less Rei is really one of a kind. i also have another theory about Rei i think that when she dose die that her memory is carried over and my only explanation is that they gather up the organic material and is infused with the rest of the dummy Rei`s. if anyone one has or had ideas about this im open for opinions

[]:             Quote Post by Lain » Thu Nov 24, 2011 2:27 pm Fuyutsuki + Gendo - Yui = Infinite Reis
